steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find circle A's intercept
steps.content: Circle A has equation $$(x+7)^2+(y-7)^2=49.$$ Its center is $(-7,7)$ and its radius is $7$. Since the center is $7$ units above the $x$-axis, the circle touches the $x$-axis at $(-7,0)$. Thus $(r,s)=(-7,0)$.

steps.title: Write circle B
steps.content: Circle B has the same radius as circle A, so its radius is also $7$. Its center is $(r,s)=(-7,0)$. A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Substitute center $(-7,0)$ and radius $7$: $$(x+7)^2+y^2=49.$$ That confirms C.
